title: Why changed-service discovery matters

problem.symptom: A build job starts without a selected service.
problem.expected: Discovery emits a concrete service matrix.

bridges.label: What the missing value means
bridges.body: The build job received no concrete service name.

bridges.label: Where the value comes from
bridges.body: The preparation step supplies each service through the matrix.

bridges.label: Where the chain broke
bridges.body: Discovery stopped before it produced that matrix.

problem.diagnosis: The discovery boundary must finish before the matrix can exist.

context.file_role: This fixture maps changed paths to buildable services.

evidence.situation: Changed paths have already been collected.
evidence.mechanism: Line 2 converts those paths into service names.
evidence.implication: The JSON matrix depends on discovery returning successfully.
evidence.gotcha: A downstream job cannot reconstruct a missing service name.

verification.claim: The checked fixture contains the narrated discovery call.
verification.result: The source hash and exact excerpt were validated.

takeaway: Service discovery is the boundary between changed files and build jobs.
